title: Future of Catholic Church involves selling their holdings & rebuilding Ancient Rome

enrichment.summary: Kyle proposes that the Catholic Church should sell all its global holdings and use the proceeds to physically rebuild ancient Rome to its peak-era layout, arguing this would realign the Church with its Roman roots, reduce institutional overhead, and create a self-sustaining tourist economy. He envisions a car-free, bicycle-based Rome filled with rebuilt colosseums, racetracks, baths, and theaters hosting modern events like gladiatorial foam-sword fights and bike races.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Should sell everything globally and reinvest in Rome
Parasitic overhead; should contract back to Rome only
Feasible and would restore historical interest and generate tourism revenue
Will dissolve into history as a subject rather than a living institution
Should be revived as foam-sword fights so participants don't get hurt
Cities and towns should cultivate distinct local flavors rather than cookie-cutter uniformity
Wasteful overhead like church infrastructure; people should be self-sufficient
Wrong; old public-domain photos should be freely usable

claims.subject: Catholic Church
claims.claim: The Catholic Church needs to sell off all of their holdings around the world.

claims.subject: Catholic Church
claims.claim: The money from selling holdings should be used to rebuild Rome.

claims.subject: Catholic Church
claims.claim: The Catholic Church is really wealthy with churches, clergy, and business interests like the papal states.

claims.subject: Rome
claims.claim: Rebuilding Rome would take people back into history and have benefits in many ways.

claims.subject: Catholic Church
claims.claim: The Catholic Church and Rome are the same thing; the Church exists because of Rome.

claims.subject: Rome
claims.claim: Rome is the best example of a world empire and should set an example for the new world.

claims.subject: future trends
claims.claim: A future trend is people identifying with their local area and creating individual flavors for towns.

claims.subject: Catholic Church
claims.claim: The Catholic Church should contract back to the city of Rome to simplify and revolutionize it.

claims.subject: economy
claims.claim: Decreasing overhead is how you keep your economy in line.

claims.subject: education
claims.claim: Having colleges just to get degrees is wasteful infrastructure that burns away the extra economy.

claims.subject: Catholic Church
claims.claim: The Catholic Church should set an example by not trying to make money off people across the world.

claims.subject: Rome
claims.claim: Rebuilding Rome as it was at its height would make it the world capital again.

claims.subject: Rome
claims.claim: Rome at its height was filled with coliseums, theaters, racetracks, and public baths.

claims.subject: Rome
claims.claim: Rebuilding Rome would bring back people's interest in history and help them memorize it.

claims.subject: future governance
claims.claim: If people memorize history, we won't need elected officials; we can all vote directly.

claims.subject: ancient technology
claims.claim: There have been amazing discoveries of ancient technology and monolithic structures.

claims.subject: tourism
claims.claim: Rebuilding ancient sites would make tourism more beautiful and historically interesting.

claims.subject: future of religion
claims.claim: The future of religion is just interesting history and the story of social structures.

claims.subject: Catholic Church
claims.claim: The Catholic Church should just be contracted back into the city of Rome with the Pontifex Maximus living there.

claims.subject: economy
claims.claim: We need a self-sustaining economy where everything supports itself without waste.

claims.subject: Catholic Church
claims.claim: The Catholic Church is currently parasitical with bad stories about shaking people down.

claims.subject: Catholic Church
claims.claim: The Catholic Church should lead the way in making outdated institutions extinct.
